machinery_genes.quote: VgpA physically interacts with VscN2 (an ATPase essential for T3SS function)
machinery_genes.note: VscN2 = T3SS2 ATPase, locus VPA1338. Gotoh 2010 Table 1 annotates VPA1338 as 'putative ATPase YscN' (SctN/YscN family), corroborating the assignment.

Named directly as the T3SS2 apparatus protein; Gotoh 2010 Table 1 annotates the corresponding ORF VPA1339 as 'putative type III secretion system EscC protein' (EscC/SctC secretin family). EscC is the unified-Sct SctC outer-membrane secretin.

machinery_genes.role: major export-apparatus ring (inner-membrane export gate; MxiA/EscV family)
machinery_genes.quote: putative type III secretion system EscV protein
machinery_genes.note: VPA1355, annotated EscV (= SctV/MxiA major export ring). This is the gene used elsewhere in the same paper as the T3SS2-secretion-defective marker vcrD2 (strain 'ΔvcrD1ΔvcrD2', 'POR-2 knockout of () gene'); vcrD2 is the V. parahaemolyticus name for the SctV/MxiA-family export apparatus gene of T3SS2.

machinery_genes.quote: putative type III secretion system EscU protein
machinery_genes.note: Locus VPA1354. EscU = unified SctU export-gate autoprotease. No separate vsc/vcr trivial name found in the literature surveyed; gene field carries the locus tag plus the EscU homology call from the annotation table.

machinery_genes.quote: putative Type III secretion protein Spa24
machinery_genes.note: Locus VPA1342. Spa24 (Shigella Mxi-Spa) = SctR export-gate component per the apparatus brief synonym table. An anti-VPA1342 antibody was raised and used in this study, confirming the protein exists and is part of the locus.

machinery_genes.quote: putative Spa29, component of the Mxi-Spa secretion machinery
machinery_genes.note: Locus VPA1341. Spa29 (Shigella Mxi-Spa) = SctT export-gate component per the apparatus brief synonym table.

machinery_genes.quote: putative Type III secretion protein Spa33
machinery_genes.note: Locus VPA1349. Spa33 (Shigella Mxi-Spa) = SctQ cytoplasmic sorting platform / C-ring per the apparatus brief synonym table.

machinery_genes.quote: putative type III secretion system lipoprotein precursor EprK
machinery_genes.note: Locus VPA1367. EprK is the EPEC/T3SS lipoprotein homologue; the inner-ring lipoprotein of an injectisome corresponds to SctJ (EscJ/PrgK/MxiJ). Assigned SctJ on the lipoprotein call; medium confidence (EprK naming is by homology in the annotation table, not by dedicated functional study).

machinery_genes.quote: we identified both VPA1362 (designated VopB2) and VPA1361 (designated VopD2) as T3SS2-dependent secretion proteins... VopB2 and VopD2 act as translocon proteins of V. parahaemolyticus T3SS2
machinery_genes.note: Locus VPA1362. Translocon (pore-forming) protein, counted as apparatus per the brief (translocon proteins form the host-membrane pore, not effectors). Gotoh 2010 Table 1 annotates VPA1362 as 'putative secreted protein EspD', consistent with a translocator homologue.

machinery_genes.quote: we identified both VPA1362 (designated VopB2) and VPA1361 (designated VopD2) as T3SS2-dependent secretion proteins... VopB2 and VopD2 act as translocon proteins of V. parahaemolyticus T3SS2
machinery_genes.note: Locus VPA1361. Second translocon protein; essential for pore formation and effector translocation by T3SS2. Counted as apparatus per the brief.

notes: T3SS-2 is the second, enterotoxicity-associated Type III secretion system of V. parahaemolyticus RIMD2210633, encoded within the ~80-kb pathogenicity island (Vp-PAI) on chromosome 2 (the VPA gene block, roughly VPA1310-VPA1393). The whole-genome paper (Makino 2003) first identified the two T3SS gene sets; Park 2004 showed both are functional secretion systems and that T3SS2 drives enterotoxicity. The strongest gene-by-gene evidence is Gotoh et al. 2010 (PLoS ONE), whose microarray Table 1 lists the Vp-PAI ORFs with their T3SS apparatus-homologue annotations and exact VPA locus tags; I lifted the apparatus components from that verbatim table (ATPase YscN/VscN2 = VPA1338, secretin EscC/VscC2 = VPA1339, Spa29/SctT = VPA1341, Spa24/SctR = VPA1342, Spa33/SctQ = VPA1349, EscU/SctU = VPA1354, EscV/SctV/vcrD2 = VPA1355, lipoprotein EprK/SctJ = VPA1367). The two translocon proteins VopB2 (VPA1362) and VopD2 (VPA1361) come from Kodama 2008, which counts as machinery under the brief (host-membrane pore). VscN2 and VscC2 trivial names are confirmed in the literature text (Lin 2021 gatekeeper paper names VscN2 as the T3SS2 ATPase; Gotoh 2010 names VscC2 as the T3SS2 apparatus protein); vcrD2 is the literature name for the SctV/MxiA export-apparatus gene (VPA1355). Status = PARTIAL rather than COMPLETE: several core Sct components are not individually named in the surveyed open-access literature, in particular the needle subunit (SctF), inner rod (SctI), inner MS-ring (SctD), the SctK/SctL ATPase-stator/C-ring base pair, the gatekeeper (here VgpA, which IS named in Lin 2021 but is a regulator-class gatekeeper and was conservatively left off the machinery list), and the export-gate SctS (Spa9). The Vp-PAI ORFs VPA1340, VPA1353 and others are annotated only as 'hypothetical' or 'outer membrane protein' in Table 1 and lack a defensible apparatus-naming quote, so they were excluded rather than guessed. Deliberately EXCLUDED as non-machinery: the secreted effectors (VopC=VPA1327/exoenzyme T, VopP/VopA=VPA1346/YopP, VopT=VPA1327 ADP-ribosyltransferase, VopL=VPA1370, OspC2/OspB-like = VPA1331/VPA1380, etc.), the TDH/TRH hemolysins (VPA1314/VPA1378), the OmpR-family transcriptional regulators VtrA (VPA1332) and VtrB (VPA1348), and chaperones (e.g. VPA1363). Lower-confidence calls: the EprK->SctJ lipoprotein assignment and the Spa-family export-gate role assignments rest on annotation-table homology names rather than dedicated structural studies.
